title: espeletia occulta subsp. occulta
intro: Espeletia occulta subsp. occulta, also known as 'Sapo' or 'Toad Flower', is a unique and endemic plant species to the Andean regions of South America, characterized by its distinctive, mottled and hairy leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Espeletia occulta subsp. occulta include its ability to reduce fever, soothe coughs, and alleviate congestion, making it a popular remedy in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve infusions or decoctions made from the leaves, which can be consumed as teas or taken as tinctures to harness its medicinal benefits.
